message.content: Adversaries may target third-party password managers to acquire user credentials, exploiting the fact that these applications store credentials in an encrypted database accessible via a master password. Once the database is unlocked, credentials can be copied to memory or extracted from files on disk. Common exploitation methods include extracting the master password or plain-text credentials from memory, often through techniques like Exploitation for Credential Access or brute forcing via Password Guessing. Notable incidents include Fox Kitten using scripts to access KeePass databases, MarkiRAT gathering information from KeePass, and TrickBot stealing passwords from KeePass. Mitigation strategies include using strong, unique master passwords, enabling multi-factor authentication (MFA) for password managers, regularly updating and patching software, and employing memory protection techniques to prevent unauthorized access.
